name: Tarsus Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
business: Tarsus Pharmaceuticals, Inc. is a commercial stage biopharmaceutical company focused on the development and commercialization of therapeutics, starting with eye care. It launched XDEMVY (lotilaner ophthalmic solution) 0.25% in August 2023 for the treatment of Demodex blepharitis, which is caused by infestation of Demodex mites. The company also develops TP-04 for ocular rosacea and TP-05 for Lyme disease prophylaxis.

exposures.sentence: We launched XDEMVY (lotilaner ophthalmic solution) 0.25%, formerly known as TP-03, for the treatment of Demodex blepharitis, in August 2023 after receiving U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approval in July 2023.

catalysts.event: Initiation of Phase 2 trial for TP-04 for ocular rosacea
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, we initiated a Phase 2 trial for the potential treatment of ocular rosacea with topline results expected in the first half of 2027.

catalysts.event: Expected Phase 2 trial initiation for TP-05 Lyme disease prophylaxis
catalysts.sentence: Given ongoing discussions with the FDA about our Lyme disease program, they agreed to our proposed approach for a Phase 2 clinical trial of TP-05 (an investigational oral tablet), which would include several hundred subjects with planned trial initiation expected in the second quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: Potential approval of preservative-free TP-03 in Europe
catalysts.sentence: In Europe, we are on track for the potential approval of a preservative-free formulation of TP-03 for the potential treatment of Demodex blepharitis expected in 2027.

catalysts.event: Potential approval of TP-03 in Greater China by partner GrandPharma
catalysts.sentence: Our partner in Greater China, GrandPharma, expects potential approval of TP-03 for Demodex blepharitis in 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 25 million people in the U.S. suffer from Demodex blepharitis.
tamClaims.sentence: There may be as many as approximately 25 million people in the U.S. who suffer from Demodex blepharitis.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 30-40 million Americans are impacted by MGD.
tamClaims.sentence: Approximately 30-40 million Americans are impacted by MGD.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 15-18 million Americans impacted by ocular rosacea.
tamClaims.sentence: Ocular rosacea is a highly prevalent disease, with approximately 15-18 million Americans impacted by ocular rosacea and there are currently no FDA-approved therapeutics to treat this disease.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 80 million people in the U.S. at risk of Lyme disease exposure.
tamClaims.sentence: There are approximately 80 million people in the U.S. at risk of Lyme disease exposure with nearly 30 million of which are moderate to high risk.
